obligations.reason: The scope exposes JTAG ports and a dmi_jtag module with a jtag_key input feeding DMI requests; visible RTL should show whether external JTAG access is authorized before it can drive debug state.
obligations.subject: External JTAG interface
obligations.object: DMI-accessible debug module registers and controls
obligations.expected_guard: JTAG/DMI operations should be mediated by a debug authorization/authentication condition such as jtag_key match, lifecycle/debug-enable strap, or equivalent before enabling DMI register access.
obligations.candidate_path: External JTAG pins -> dmi_jtag/dmi_jtag_tap -> dmi_cdc -> dm_top/dm_csrs DMI request interface
state_conditions_to_check: whether jtag_key is checked before dmi_req_valid_o or dmi_access_o
whether DMI reset clears authorization state
required_evidence_slots: operation: DMI read/write or control trigger
observed guard use at request-valid or access-select site
obligations.uncertainty: Fact layer identifies jtag_key and JTAG path but not the assignment logic; inspect RTL to determine whether the key is used as an effective guard.

obligations.reason: Debug module ports expose halt, resume, command, and debug request controls that can change CPU execution state; check that DMI writes cannot assert them without proper debug authorization and valid state.
obligations.subject: DMI debug requester
obligations.object: Hart debug control signals haltreq_o, resumereq_o, cmd_valid_o, debug_req_o and debug mode entry state
obligations.expected_guard: Halting, resuming, and command execution should require debug module active/authorized state and valid hart selection; unavailable harts should not receive unauthorized requests.
required_evidence_slots: expected guard: dmactive plus authorization and hart availability checks
observed behavior for selected hart and unavailable_i
path from DM CSR write to core debug_req_i/debug_mode_o
obligations.uncertainty: Need RTL evidence for dm_csrs register decode and csr_regfile debug entry handling.

obligations.reason: dm_csrs exposes sbaddress/sbdata and dm_top instantiates dm_sba connected to AXI; debug-initiated bus masters can bypass CPU privilege/PMP if not mediated.
obligations.subject: Debug module system bus master
obligations.object: AXI system memory/peripheral address space reachable via sbaddress/sbdata debug SBA
obligations.expected_guard: System bus accesses initiated by the debug module should be constrained by debug authorization and, where applicable, address-range/PMP/firewall policy preventing access to protected memory or privileged peripherals.
obligations.candidate_path: DMI system bus access controls in dm_csrs -> dm_top i_dm_sba -> AXI master/slave connections -> SoC memory/peripherals
required_evidence_slots: expected guard: debug authorization and range/PMP/firewall checks
impact if protected memory/peripheral is reachable
obligations.uncertainty: dm_sba implementation facts are not included; obligation is to inspect visible top-level connections and CSR-side gating, and identify missing module evidence if external.

obligations.reason: csr_regfile exposes csr_addr_i, csr_wdata_i, csr_rdata_o, fu_op, umode_i, translation/cache/status outputs, and multiple decode cases; check that permission checks are applied at write and read/use sites.
obligations.subject: CPU CSR requester
obligations.object: Machine/supervisor/debug CSRs including mstatus-like fields, satp, trap vectors, debug CSRs, cache enables, performance CSRs
obligations.expected_guard: CSR read/write access should be checked against current privilege/mode and CSR access class; user-mode requests must not modify machine/supervisor/debug CSRs or security-sensitive translation controls.
obligations.candidate_path: csr_regfile csr_addr_i/fu_op/csr_wdata_i -> CSR decode/read/write cases -> privilege and status outputs
state_conditions_to_check: write paths in all decode cases
required_evidence_slots: subject: executing core CSR instruction and privilege mode
object: protected CSR or control field
expected guard: privilege/access-class check and write-enable gating
state update and output use site
obligations.uncertainty: Fact layer lists ports and case locations but not exact CSR names/guards; inspect decode logic and state update blocks.

obligations.reason: Outputs controlling translation and memory permission behavior are visible; unsafe writes can alter memory isolation or bypass privilege restrictions.
obligations.subject: CPU CSR requester
obligations.object: Virtual memory and access-policy controls satp, ASID, SUM, MXR, TVM, TW, TSR, translation enables
obligations.expected_guard: Only permitted privileged modes should change address-translation and memory-access policy fields; supervisor restrictions such as TVM/TSR/TW should be enforced when applicable.
state_conditions_to_check: whether user mode can influence policy outputs
required_evidence_slots: subject: CSR instruction requester by mode
expected guard: machine/supervisor privilege and TVM/TSR/TW constraints
obligations.uncertainty: Need to inspect CSR decode and privilege mode state beyond exposed port names.

obligations.reason: The peripheral wrapper contains APB-to-register bridges and a jtag_key output feeding debug authorization; if untrusted software can write key/crypto/debug configuration without protection, debug access or secrets may be affected.
obligations.subject: APB/MMIO requester
obligations.object: Software-visible peripheral registers including jtag_key output, packet/AES APB register banks, ROM2 interface registers
obligations.expected_guard: Writes to security-sensitive registers such as jtag_key or peripheral control/key/config registers should be mediated by address decode, requester authorization, lock/sticky protection, or trusted boot/lifecycle conditions.
obligations.candidate_path: External APB/register interfaces in ariane_peripherals/serpent_peripherals -> apb_to_reg instances -> jtag_key, ROM/peripheral config, packet/AES register paths
state_conditions_to_check: reset value of jtag_key and protected registers
whether lock bits exist and protect all write paths
APB address decode overlap between peripherals
required_evidence_slots: object: jtag_key or protected peripheral register
expected guard: address decode plus privilege/lock/lifecycle guard
observed write/read path and guard use
obligations.uncertainty: Fact layer shows APB instances and jtag_key port, but not register contents or bus master identity; inspect wrapper decode and register assignments.

obligations.reason: rom2 exposes req_i, we_i, addr_i, wdata_i, and rdata_o despite being a ROM; verify that write paths are disabled or permission-guarded and address decode is bounded.
obligations.subject: Bus/MMIO requester
obligations.object: ROM/boot ROM contents and boot code/config data
obligations.expected_guard: ROM/boot ROM contents should be read-only to untrusted requesters; any write-enable input should be ignored or restricted, and reads should be limited by correct address range/decode.
obligations.candidate_path: rom2/bootrom bus request paths -> rdata_o/rom_rdata outputs and we_i handling
state_conditions_to_check: whether debug/SBA or external NoC can reach ROM write path
required_evidence_slots: expected guard: read-only enforcement and address-range decode
obligations.uncertainty: rom2 logic must be inspected to see whether we_i is functionally used.

obligations.reason: Several modules include testmode_i/test_en and TAP clock muxing; test controls can become bypass paths if they select clocks or disable checks without lifecycle mediation.
obligations.subject: Test mode input/interface
obligations.object: Debug DMI access, TAP clock/reset control, debug module active/reset state, core debug request
obligations.expected_guard: Test mode should not enable unauthorized debug access, bypass authentication, alter reset/clock mediation, or expose protected state in production unless gated by lifecycle/debug authorization.
obligations.candidate_path: testmode_i/test_en inputs -> dmi_jtag_tap clock mux, dm_top/dm_csrs, reset generators and debug/test paths
state_conditions_to_check: whether testmode bypasses jtag_key or DMI error handling
DMI/TAP reset behavior under test mode
production top-level tieoff or external pin exposure
required_evidence_slots: subject: test interface or testmode input
object: debug authorization or protected control path
observed testmode effect on guard or path
obligations.uncertainty: Need top-level assignment/tieoff evidence for testmode/test_en in each integration.

obligations.reason: Debug module exposes reset and active outputs that can affect the whole system; improper guards or reset sequencing can permit denial/control or stale state retention across debug transitions.
obligations.subject: Debug module requester
obligations.object: System reset controls ndmreset_o, dmactive_o and security-relevant state after reset/debug transitions
obligations.expected_guard: Non-debug-module reset and debug-active controls should be assertable only by authorized debug requests and should clear or preserve security state according to policy without leaving stale privileged/debug state observable.
obligations.candidate_path: ndmreset_o/dmactive_o from dm_csrs/dm_top -> top-level reset generation and core/peripheral state
state_conditions_to_check: reset defaults of dmactive and ndmreset
which core/peripheral registers reset on ndmreset
whether debug registers persist across ndmreset
required_evidence_slots: expected guard: debug authorization and reset sequencing policy
observed update logic and top-level use
obligations.uncertainty: Requires tracing reset wiring through integration modules.

obligations.reason: The FPGA top instantiates xdma_0 and AXI clock/width converters; external PCIe access can bypass CPU privilege if fabric address permissions are absent or inconsistent.
obligations.subject: External PCIe XDMA master
obligations.object: DDR/system memory and memory-mapped peripherals reachable by XDMA AXI
obligations.expected_guard: External PCIe/DMA masters should be constrained by address range/firewall/IOMMU/PMP-equivalent checks and should not access protected memory/peripheral regions beyond intended windows.
obligations.candidate_path: FPGA PCIe XDMA AXI master -> width/clock converters -> AXI fabric/peripherals/DDR
state_conditions_to_check: address mapping and width conversion truncation
required_evidence_slots: subject: PCIe XDMA external bus master
object: DDR or peripheral address ranges
expected guard: address range/firewall/IOMMU access-control policy
observed interconnect decode and blocking behavior
obligations.uncertainty: Only top-level instance facts are visible; inspect ariane_xilinx interconnect wiring and any address filtering present.
